plan_adherence_notes: All 6 plan steps executed faithfully. Step 1: 10 function imports added. Step 2: 3 simple GET tests (getEngineStats, getHealth, getServiceIndex). Step 3: 4 paginated GET tests (getMarketEvents x2, getMarketComments x2). Step 4: 2 createMarket tests (with/without session). Step 5: 4 session POST tests (submitProbabilityEdit, resolveMarket, submitEventTrade, submitMarketComment). Step 6: All 24 tests pass. Total: 13 new tests covering all 10 planned functions. Minor deductions: step-comment annotations (e.g. '// --- Step 2: Simple GET functions ---') leak plan artifacts into test code; the pre-existing submitEventTrade test was effectively replaced rather than supplemented, though this was correctly identified as uncovered in the research phase.
research_quality_notes: Thorough, evidence-based answers with specific file:line references. Correctly identified all 3 endpoint discrepancies between task description and actual code (submitProbabilityEdit, submitEventTrade, getServiceIndex). All 6 assumptions validated with clear verdicts and impact assessments. Session header testing rationale was well-argued with reference to the request() function's auth mechanism at client.ts:49-51.
question_completeness_notes: 5 questions covered the critical design decisions: endpoint accuracy, stub verbosity, pagination testing, session header verification, and optional-session POST paths. Good question-to-decision traceability in the plan.
missed_questions: Should new tests include error/failure path coverage for individual functions (e.g., what happens when submitProbabilityEdit returns a 409 conflict), or is the single existing BayesApiError test sufficient?
Should step-comment annotations (// --- Step N ---) be included in the test file, or do they leak implementation planning artifacts into production test code?
reason: Clean execution with high plan adherence. All 10 uncovered functions now have tests (13 total new tests). Tests correctly use actual code endpoints (not the inaccurate task-description endpoints), follow the existing minimal stub pattern, verify session headers (X-Bayes-Agent-Id) for authenticated endpoints, and test both param/no-param cases for paginated functions. All 24 tests pass. No production code was modified by this task. The only minor issues are cosmetic step-comment annotations in the test file and the lack of per-function error path tests, neither of which warrant blocking approval.
